data.title: FlexShares announces liquidation of FlexShares International Quality Dividend Defensive Index Fund
data.description:
• Northern Trust’s FlexShares Exchange Traded Funds (NASDAQ:NTRS) announced the liquidation and termination of the FlexShares International Quality Dividend Defensive Index Fund (IQDE).
  • Prior to and through the close of regular trading on the NYSE Arca on or about January 21, 2025, the Fund will undertake the process of closing down and liquidating its portfolio.
  • The Fund will discontinue accepting orders for the purchase of creation units after 3:00 p.m., Eastern time, on January 17, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Energroup Holdings to acquire Cocannco
data.description:
• Energroup Holdings (OTC:ENHD) on Friday announced plans to acquire Cocannco.
  • This transaction would transform Energroup into a revenue-generating company and remove its current "shell" status, it said.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Jeffs’ Brands signs LOI to merge Fort Products with Impact Acquisitions valued at up to $12M
data.description:
• Jeffs' Brands (NASDAQ:JFBR) said on Friday it has signed a non-binding Letter of Intent for the potential merge of Fort Products, a UK-based private company, with Impact Acquisitions Corp.
  • Under the proposed share exchange agreement, Impact Acquisitions will acquire 100% of Fort Products’ shares. Jeffs Brands will receive between 75.02% and 83.29% ownership of Impact share capital, contingent upon meeting predetermined milestones.
  • The proposed acquisition is based on a total value of Impact of approximately 4.85M CAD (approximately 3.37M$US) and a total value ascribed to Fort Products, of approximately 17.12M CAD (approximately 11.91M$US).
  • Impact's board of directors will order a valuation of Fort Products, and a condition for the proposed transaction is that the value of Fort Products will not be less than a minimum amount of 14M CAD (approximately 9.73M$US).
  • Jeffs' Brands (JFBR) shares were up more than 18% in pre-market trading on Friday.

data.title: Rivian Automotive gains after topping Q4 deliveries expectations
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN) announced on Friday that it produced 12,727 vehicles at its manufacturing facility in Normal, Illinois in Q4 and delivered 14,183 vehicles during the same period. Both those marks were ahead of the consensus estimate of analysts.

On a full-year 2024 basis, the company produced 49,476 vehicles and delivered 51,579, which was roughly in line with management’s full-year 2024 guidance of 47,000 to 49,000 vehicles produced and 50,500 to 52,000 vehicles delivered.

The electric vehicle maker highlighted that the shortage of a shared component on the R1 and RCV platforms is no longer a constraint on its production.

Rivian Automotive (RIVN) plans to release its Q4 earnings report on February 20.

Shares of Rivian (RIVN) pushed 3.2% higher in premarket action on Friday.

data.title: Apple App Store sees 13% growth in December, 15% growth in Q1: BofA
data.description:
Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) App Store saw nearly 13% revenue growth in the month of December and 15% growth in the tech giant's fiscal first-quarter, Bank of America said on Friday, citing data from research firm SensorTower.

Apple shares fell 0.5% in premarket trading.

Revenue for the App Store rose 12.7% year-over-year increase globally, as areas like the U.S. (14% year-over-year growth) and the European Union (29% year-over-year growth) aided the overall figure, Bank of America analyst Wamsi Mohan wrote in a note to clients, citing the SensorTower data.

Turkey and Thailand also saw strong growth in the fiscal first-quarter, up 90% and 46% year-over-year, respectively.

And even China, where Apple is dealing with hardware problems, saw 7.2% year-over-year growth, Mohan added. Mohan has a Buy rating and $256 price target on Apple.

Breakdown

Breaking it down by category, entertainment apps — Apple's second largest — remained strong, as revenue surged 37% year-over-year, Mohan said. Revenue from gaming — the largest category — rose 5% year-over-year to $3.8B and accounted for roughly 48% of total App Store revenue, down from 53% in the year-ago period, Mohan added.

Rounding out the top categories were Photo & Video, which accounted for 7% of revenue; Social Networking at 4%; Music at 3%; and Other at 21%.

Productivity apps were the fastest growing category, up 48% year-over-year, due in large part to ChatGPT, Mohan said.

Total iPhone and iPad downloads rose 7.6% year-over-year to come in at roughly 8.8B.

data.title: Innovation Beverage appoints Sahil Beri as interim CEO, expands into eastern U.S.
data.description:
• Innovation Beverage Group (NASDAQ:IBG) has announced that its Chairman and COO, Sahil Beri, has been appointed Interim CEO while the Co. conducts a search for a new CEO.
  • Dean Huge, the Co.'s former CEO, has resigned to pursue other interests.
  • Under Mr. Beri's leadership, IBG is poised for an exciting phase of growth, beginning with its expansion plans in the Eastern U.S.
  • The Co. had recently announced a Sales Services Agreement with Atlanta, Georgia-based Blue Ridge Spirits & Wine Marketing to sell its Australian Bitters Co.
  • "The Eastern U.S. rollout is part of our broader strategy to establish a foothold in key markets across North America. Investors can look forward to a promising year in 2025 as we continue to expand our presence and meet the growing demand for high-quality cocktail products," Mr. Beri added.
  • IBG -4.52% premarket to $1.07.

data.title: Dividend Roundup: Johnson & Johnson, JPMorgan, Oracle, Mastercard, and more
data.description:

Additionally, looking towards next week, industry players such as JPMorgan (NYSE:JPM) and Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) will see the ex-dividend dates for their upcoming dividend payments.

See a breakdown below of some of the highlights of this week, along with some upcoming activity on the dividend front.

Upcoming Dividend Calendar

Oracle (ORCL)

  • Payout date: January 23

data.title: Ardent Health gains on acquisition of 18 urgent care clinics
data.description:
• Healthcare provider Ardent Health (NYSE:ARDT) added ~6% in the premarket on Friday after announcing the acquisition of 18 urgent care clinics in New Mexico and Oklahoma from NextCare Urgent Care.
  • As part of the transaction, six urgent care clinics in New Mexico and 12 clinics in Oklahoma will join Ardent's (NYSE:ARDT) healthcare network, operating under its Lovelace Health System and Hillcrest HealthCare System, respectively.
  • ARDT operates five hospitals and 25 sites of care in New Mexico and eight hospitals and 57 sites of care in Oklahoma.
  • The deal, financial terms of which were not provided, "significantly expands Ardent Health's ambulatory operations in both markets," the Brentwood, Tenn.-based firm said.
  • The transaction follows ARDT's acquisition of nine urgent care centers in its East Texas and Topeka, Kansas, markets in 2024.

data.title: RPM declares $0.51 dividend
data.description:

  • See RPM Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Anthropic reaches deal with music publishers in copyright lawsuit over AI models - report
data.description:
Anthropic has reached an agreement to settle parts of a copyright infringement lawsuit ​​brought by music publishers over alleged training of the company's AI models on protected song lyrics, The Verge reported.

The agreement, which was signed by U.S. District Judge Eumi, requires Anthropic to apply existing guardrails in the training of future AI models and to set up a procedure for music publishers to intervene when copyright infringement is suspected, the report added, citing a court document.

In October 2023, music publishers including Universal Music Group (OTCPK:UMGNF) (OTCPK:UNVGY) and Concord Music filed a copyright infringement lawsuit against Anthropic for allegedly training its AI model on lyrics from at least 500 protected songs, the report noted.

Anthropic, Universal Music Group, and Concord Music have not yet responded to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

The music publishers acknowledged that platforms such as Genius already distribute lyrics online, however noted that these websites pay a licensing fee to use the copyrighted songs. Anthropic “intentionally removed or altered copyright management information” from the impacted songs while ingesting data found on those websites to train its AI models, the report added.

Under the agreement, Anthropic noted that it would maintain its already implemented guardrails in its current AI models and products, which intend to prevent its AI models from infringing on copyrighted content. The company will also apply its existing guardrails to any future AI systems it develops, according to the court filing.

At any time during the pendency of the proceeding, the music publishers can notify Anthropic if the guardrails are not effectively preventing the output that reproduces the music lyrics controlled by the publishers. Anthropic would need to respond to the publishers and undertake an investigation into the allegations, and the publishers will cooperate in good faith, as per the court filing.

Claude "isn't designed to be used for copyright infringement, and we have numerous processes in place designed to prevent such infringement,"” Anthropic said in a statement to The Hollywood Reporter.

"We continue to look forward to showing that, consistent with existing copyright law, using potentially copyrighted material in the training of generative AI models is a quintessential fair use," added Anthropic — which is backed by Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and Alphabet's (GOOGL) (GOOG) Google — the developer of AI model Claude.

The music publishers behind the original complaint have asked for a preliminary injunction to restrict Anthropic from training future AI models on their protected song lyrics. The court is expected to decide in the coming months, the report added.
